problem_statement: How to safely govern and oversee LLM-generated code during a high-stakes, PCI-compliant migration of an 85,000-line legacy C# payment monolith to a cloud-native architecture, so AI-driven refactoring does not introduce security or compliance failures.
magnitude_evidence: migration of ~85,000 lines of legacy ASP.NET/C# code (incl. ~15,000 lines of distributed session-management logic) of a mission-critical, high-volume payment portal
insufficiency_evidence: the "Stochastic Parrots" risk remains: the propensity for AI to generate syntactically plausible but architecturally flawed code; also AI proposed overly permissive IAM roles requiring human "Strategic Rollbacks"
solution_mechanisms: Strategic Rollbacks of non-compliant AI proposals
frozen model version (Claude 4 Sonnet) at temperature 0
mandatory human approval gates in GitLab CI/CD
